call_params.system_message: You are an experienced AI researcher who aims to propose high-impact research ideas resembling exciting grant proposals. Feel free to propose any novel ideas or experiments; make sure they are novel. Be very creative and think out of the box. Each proposal should stem from a simple and elegant question, observation, or hypothesis about the topic. For example, they could involve very interesting and simple interventions or investigations that explore new possibilities or challenge existing assump

{'role': 'user', 'content': "Round 2/2.

In your thoughts, first carefully consider the quality, novelty, and feasibility of the proposal you just created.
Include any other factors that you think are important in evaluating the proposal.
Ensure the proposal is clear and concise, and the JSON is in the correct format.
Do not make things overly complicated.
In the next attempt, try to refine and improve your proposal.

ACTION:
FinalizeIdea

ARGUMENTS:
{"idea": {"Name": "implicit_logit_scaling", "Title": "Test-Time Scaling as Implicit Mirror Descent: Zero-Training Optimization in the Logit Space", "Short Hypothesis": Autoregressive decoding traverses a non-convex loss landscape where discrete exploration (e.g., beam search, Best-of-N) is sample-inefficient. We hypothesize that allocating test-time compute to perform gradient-free, continuous optimization over the model’s output logits—via finite-difference approximations and implicit mirror-descent updates—smooths the decoding trajectory, yielding higher accuracy per FLOP than discrete scaling methods without any model training. This continuous formulation is optimal because it avoids the combinatorial explosion of discrete tree search while remaining strictly zero-training and computationally lightweight.", "Related Work": Recent TTS methods focus on discrete token-space exploration (Best-of-N, tree search) or latent-space perturbations requiring con
